graphics.name: null
graphics.notes: Use NullRenderer and NullRhiDevice for deterministic generated-game and engine tests without GPU or window dependencies, including backend-neutral runtime texture upload, mesh vertex/index buffer upload, and material descriptor binding coverage.

graphics.name: d3d12
graphics.notes: Visible clear/draw/readback, swapchain lifecycle, Win32 desktop game-window RhiFrameRenderer creation from host-supplied precompiled shader bytecode, installed packaged cooked-scene GPU binding validation with target-specific scene DXIL artifacts, installed depth-aware Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 validation with target-specific postprocess DXIL artifacts and postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, installed selected D3D12 height-fog package evidence with --require-environment-fog-evidence, environment_fog_status=ready, environment_fog_constants_binding=4, environment_fog_constants_byte_size=256, postprocess_policy_fog_effect=1, and postprocess_d3d12_execution_passes_ok=1, installed package-visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 validation with target-specific shadow receiver/shadow DXIL artifacts, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, selected D3D12 instanced draw execution validation with --require-scene-scale-policy --require-d3d12-instanced-draw-evidence, scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_required=1, scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_ready=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_status=ready, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_ready=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_selected=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_expected_instances=6, positive d3d12_instanced_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_instances_submitted, d3d12_instanced_draws_ok=1, d3d12_instanced_instances_ok=1, positive rhi_instanced_draw_calls, positive rhi_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, and positive rhi_instanced_instances_submitted, plus Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 validation with --require-renderer-quality-gates and Renderer General Quality Matrix v1 package counters with renderer_quality_matrix_status=host_evidence_required, renderer_quality_matrix_dependency_gated_rows=0, renderer_quality_matrix_unsupported_rows=0, renderer_quality_matrix_d3d12_ready=1, renderer_quality_matrix_vulkan_strict_ready=1, renderer_quality_matrix_metal_ready=0, and renderer_quality_matrix_general_renderer_quality_ready=0, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=6, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=15, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok=1, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1 are implemented through backend-private Direct3D 12 ownership.

graphics.name: vulkan
graphics.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V draw/readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V texture-sampling proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V sampled-depth readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V postprocess-depth readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV, and MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV for real SPIR-V shadow receiver readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV for real SPIR-V compute dispatch readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph POSITION readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph renderer-consumption proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV for Runtime RHI compute morph NORMAL/TANGENT readback proof; configure MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV for cooked scene GPU palette proof
graphics.notes: Runtime owners and the IRhiDevice bridge cover backend-neutral resource/shader/descriptor/pipeline/swapchain creation, standalone texture draw/readback, swapchain clear/present, command submission, fence stats, readback-domain buffer reads, private depth image-view/layout/barrier/dynamic-rendering depth attachments, required synchronization2 feature enablement, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, capability-gated timestamp query pool writes/readback stats, and actual surface capability/format/present-mode queries before native swapchain creation. Win32/Vulkan game-window RhiFrameRenderer creation, host-owned cooked-scene GPU binding injection, depth-aware Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 package proof from precompiled SPIR-V artifacts with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, package-visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 proof with target-specific shadow receiver/shadow SPIR-V artifacts, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, plus Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 validation with --require-renderer-quality-gates and Renderer General Quality Matrix v1 package counters with renderer_quality_matrix_status=host_evidence_required, renderer_quality_matrix_dependency_gated_rows=0, renderer_quality_matrix_unsupported_rows=0, renderer_quality_matrix_d3d12_ready=1, renderer_quality_matrix_vulkan_strict_ready=1, renderer_quality_matrix_metal_ready=0, and renderer_quality_matrix_general_renderer_quality_ready=0, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=6, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=15, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok=1, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1 is validated on this Windows host when Vulkan runtime, surface support, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and spirv-val are available; Vulkan debug profiling timestamp readiness additionally requires positive timestamp frequency, query writes, query-result readback, and zero query failures. Linux and generated-game Vulkan availability remain host/toolchain gated. The visible draw/readback, texture-sampling, runtime material sampling, cooked-scene palette, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, postprocess-depth readback, and shadow receiver readback proofs are environment-gated by local Vulkan runtime plus real SPIR-V artifacts.

graphics.name: metal
graphics.notes: Native MTLDevice, command queue, command buffer, render encoder, texture target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture readback ownership are implemented behind Apple-only Objective-C++ PIMPL boundaries; shader library creation and visible backend integration remain Apple-toolchain-gated roadmap work.

audio.name: device-independent
audio.notes: Gameplay cue/bus/fade/pause/loop/trigger planning through plan_gameplay_audio_mix, mixer scheduling, spatial voice/listener attenuation and pan metadata, decoded streaming queue append/consume/starvation state, streaming buffer state, format conversion intent, underrun diagnostics, selectable nearest/linear resampling, deterministic interleaved float PCM generation, and bounded device queue-fill planning through plan_audio_device_stream / render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float are validated without opening an OS audio device. Audio Production Playback/Streaming/DSP/Spatialization Evidence v1 adds review_audio_production_readiness for reviewed decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, budget, DSP graph, spatial/listener, HRTF host-gate, device lifecycle, unsupported claim, side-effect, selected-package, production-readiness, diagnostic, and replay-hash rows. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package emit audio_production_* counters with selected package evidence ready while device and HRTF evidence remain host-gated; broad codec support, middleware parity, HRTF execution parity, background streaming execution, native handles, and general production audio quality remain unclaimed.

audio.name: wasapi-shared-mode
audio.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_audio_tests MK_wasapi_audio_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_audio_tests|MK_wasapi_audio_tests"
audio.notes: Windows-native adapter plans COM startup, default render endpoint use, shared-mode IAudioClient stream setup, render-client queue writes, silence packets, pause/resume/clear controls, and callback-free lifecycle evidence while COM and WASAPI handles remain private. It is not exclusive-mode audio, capture, device-selection UI, spatial audio, hotplug parity, broad latency readiness, or cross-platform audio parity.

ui.name: MK_ui
ui.notes: Generated runtime HUD/menu code must target first-party retained-mode mirakana::ui contracts, including the headless layout solver, build_renderer_submission payload path, host-independent strict-UTF-8 text shaping request validation plus scalar-boundary TextLayoutRun evidence through plan_text_shaping_request / shape_text_run before ITextShapingAdapter dispatch, separate TextLineBreakRun rows for ILineBreakingAdapter evidence without fabricated shaping glyph rows, host-independent font rasterization request validation through plan_font_rasterization_request / rasterize_font_glyph, host-independent platform text-input session begin/end validation through plan_platform_text_input_session / begin_platform_text_input / plan_platform_text_input_end / end_platform_text_input before IPlatformIntegrationAdapter dispatch, host-independent committed text edit validation through plan_committed_text_input / apply_committed_text_input, host-independent text edit command validation through plan_text_edit_command / apply_text_edit_command for scalar-boundary cursor movement and backward/forward deletion, host-independent text edit clipboard command validation through plan_text_edit_clipboard_command / apply_text_edit_clipboard_command for copy/cut/paste command application over caller-owned selection state through IClipboardTextAdapter, host-independent clipboard text validation through plan_clipboard_text_write / write_clipboard_text / plan_clipboard_text_read / read_clipboard_text before IClipboardTextAdapter dispatch, optional platform key-row and clipboard shortcut conversion outside MK_ui, host-independent image decode request validation through plan_image_decode_request / decode_image_request before IImageDecodingAdapter dispatch, and MK_ui_renderer UiRendererImagePalette colored sprite placeholder adapter with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup for image-row smoke tests; production text shaping implementation, real fonts, native IME sessions, platform key mapping beyond the narrow reviewed text-edit command and clipboard-shortcut bridges, key repeat policy, full text editing widgets, selection UI, full copy/cut/paste shortcut/widget behavior, rich clipboard formats, accessibility bridges, runtime image decoding, source image codecs, SVG/vector parsing, renderer texture upload, concrete texture/material renderer adapters, and platform integration remain adapter boundaries.

ui.name: runtime-ui-standard-widgets
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_ui_standard_widgets_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R MK_runtime_ui_standard_widgets_tests; selected 2D/3D desktop-runtime package smoke with --require-runtime-ui-standard-widgets
ui.notes: First-Party Runtime UI Standard Widgets v1 adds RuntimeUiStandardWidgetProvenanceDesc, RuntimeUiStandardWidgetProvenancePlan, RuntimeUiMeterDesc, RuntimeUiMeterPlan, RuntimeUiMenuStackDesc, RuntimeUiMenuStackPlan, RuntimeUiStandardHudDesc, RuntimeUiStandardHudPlan, review_runtime_ui_standard_widget_provenance, plan_runtime_ui_meters, build_runtime_ui_meter_document, plan_runtime_ui_menu_stack, build_runtime_ui_menu_stack_document, and plan_runtime_ui_standard_hud for first-party HUD meters, pause/settings menu stacks, localization/accessibility/style-token metadata, and UiDocument composition. The provenance gate treats official Unity, Unreal Engine, and Godot documentation only as category research and rejects copied external expression, external engine code/assets, external engine public UI system names, marketplace assets, and UI middleware references before a standard HUD plan can become ready. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package can require --require-runtime-ui-standard-widgets and report runtime_ui_standard_widgets_ready=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_provenance_ready=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_official_documentation_rows=3, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_first_party_design_rows=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_meter_rows=3, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_menu_screens=2, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_menu_actions=4, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_document_elements=19, runtime_ui_meter_health_normalized=0.750, runtime_ui_meter_mana_normalized=0.400, runtime_ui_meter_stamina_normalized=0.100, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_health_accessibility_ready=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_mana_localization_ready=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_stamina_warning_style_ready=1, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_external_engine_code=0, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_external_engine_assets=0, runtime_ui_standard_widgets_ui_middleware=0, and runtime_ui_standard_widgets_diagnostics=0. This is selected value-model and package-visible smoke evidence only; it does not claim copied Unity/Unreal/Godot behavior, source, assets, API names, theme/layout parity, UI middleware adoption, production text shaping, real font loading/rasterization, native IME, OS accessibility publication, renderer texture upload execution, or broad platform UI parity.

ui.name: runtime-ui-widgets
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_ui_widgets_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R MK_runtime_ui_widgets_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset desktop-runtime --target sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; out/build/desktop-runtime/games/Debug/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.exe --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.geindex --require-runtime-ui-widgets
ui.notes: Runtime Widget Vocabulary v1 adds RuntimeUiWidgetKind, RuntimeUiWidgetPlanStatus, RuntimeUiWidgetDiagnosticCode, RuntimeUiWidgetDiagnostic, RuntimeUiWidgetRow, RuntimeUiWidgetStateRow, RuntimeUiWidgetCommandRow, RuntimeUiWidgetPlan, runtime_ui_widget_kind_name, runtime_ui_widget_plan_status_name, and plan_runtime_ui_widgets for first-party button, toggle, slider, text_field, menu_stack, modal_layer, list, tree, hud_prompt, controller_glyph, state, command, and focusable-counter rows. The planner fails closed for missing or duplicate widget ids, missing labels, invalid slider ranges, controller glyph rows without input source ids, missing state or command targets, duplicate state or command ids, missing command ids, public native handle flags, UI middleware tokens, and native/external tokens. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package can require --require-runtime-ui-widgets and report runtime_ui_widgets_status=ready, runtime_ui_widgets_ready=1, runtime_ui_widget_rows=10, runtime_ui_widget_command_rows=2, runtime_ui_widget_focusable_rows=6, and runtime_ui_widget_diagnostics=0. This is selected value-model and package-visible vocabulary evidence only; it does not render UI, execute gameplay commands, shape text, rasterize fonts, start native IME sessions, publish OS accessibility, upload renderer textures, adopt UI middleware, expose native handles, or claim Unity/Unreal/Godot compatibility or parity.

ui.name: runtime-ui-binding-input-routing

This is selected value-model and package-visible binding/routing evidence only; it does not create a visible UI editor, render UI, execute gameplay commands, shape text, rasterize fonts, start native IME sessions, publish OS accessibility, upload renderer textures, adopt UI middleware, expose native handles, or claim Unity/Unreal/Godot compatibility or parity.

ui.name: runtime-ui-authoring-documents
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_ui_authoring_tests sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R MK_runtime_ui_authoring_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package
ui.notes: First-Party UI Authoring Documents v1 adds RuntimeUiAuthoringDocument, RuntimeUiThemeDocument, RuntimeUiAuthoredElementRow, RuntimeUiAuthoredWidgetRow, RuntimeUiAuthoredBindingRow, RuntimeUiThemeTokenRow, RuntimeUiAuthoringDiagnostic, RuntimeUiAuthoringDocumentParseResult, RuntimeUiThemeParseResult, runtime_ui_theme_token_kind_name, parse_runtime_ui_document, write_runtime_ui_document, parse_runtime_ui_theme, and write_runtime_ui_theme for deterministic GameEngine.UiDocument.v1 and GameEngine.UiTheme.v1 line-based authoring files. MK_runtime_ui_authoring_tests covers parse/write round-trip, duplicate element ids, unknown parent ids, invalid theme tokens, unsafe path/native/middleware/external-engine tokens, missing localization keys, missing accessibility names, and unknown widget ids. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package runtimePackageFiles include runtime/ui/main_menu.uidoc and runtime/ui/default.uitheme. This is first-party source authoring format and package-file evidence only; it does not adopt CSS/web semantics, Unity/Unreal/Godot API or visual parity, UI middleware, visible editor execution, renderer upload, text shaping, font rasterization, native IME, OS accessibility publication, package script execution, or native handle exposure.

ui.name: runtime-ui-production-stack-evidence
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package
ui.notes: Runtime UI Production Stack Evidence v1 adds RuntimeUiProductionEvidenceRow, RuntimeUiProductionStackRequest, RuntimeUiProductionStackPlan, and plan_runtime_ui_production_stack for side-effect-free text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas, renderer handoff, IME, and accessibility evidence rows. The shaping/raster gate now requires public value evidence for shaping direction/script/language segment rows, glyph ids/clusters/advances/offsets, fallback rows, bidi and line-break boundaries, glyph bitmap/metrics/pixel-format rows, atlas placement/budget/eviction rows, renderer upload handoff, dependency gates, host gates, and unsupported-claim rows before readiness can be promoted. The IME gate requires session begin/end, composition update, candidate, text-area/cursor, committed-text, clipboard, selected platform adapter proof, per-platform host gate, and dispatch-boundary rows. The accessibility gate requires role/name/description/state/focus/action/relationship/live-region/keyboard-pattern/publication-status rows, Microsoft UI Automation host gate rows, and per-platform accessibility host gate rows. The plan distinguishes ready, host-gated, dependency-gated, skipped, adapter-invoked, and unsupported rows so missing dependency evidence, skipped evidence, platform-host-gated IME evidence, UIA-only accessibility evidence, adapter side effects, and broad unsupported claims cannot be promoted to production readiness. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-runtime-ui-production-stack validates runtime_ui_production_stack_status=host_evidence_required, six rows, four ready rows, two host-gated rows, zero dependency-gated/skipped/adapter-invoked/unsupported rows, selected package evidence, runtime_ui_production_stack_ime_platform_adapter_proof_rows=1, runtime_ui_production_stack_ime_platform_host_gate_rows=1, runtime_ui_production_stack_ime_platform_parity_ready=0, runtime_ui_production_stack_accessibility_uia_host_gate_rows=1, runtime_ui_production_stack_accessibility_platform_host_gate_rows=1, runtime_ui_production_stack_accessibility_platform_parity_ready=0, zero adapter/native/renderer-upload invocation counters, and a positive replay hash. It is selected package evidence only, not production text shaping, real font loading/rasterization, native IME parity, OS accessibility publication, renderer texture upload execution, UI middleware adoption, or broad platform UI parity.

ui.name: runtime-ui-platform-production-gate
ui.notes: Runtime UI Platform Production Gate v1 adds RuntimeUiPlatformProductionFeature, RuntimeUiPlatformProductionProofKind, RuntimeUiPlatformProductionEvidenceRow, RuntimeUiPlatformProductionResult, and evaluate_runtime_ui_platform_production for a single fail-closed UI platform readiness evaluator. Required feature rows are visible_ui_editor, production_text_shaping, real_font_loading, font_rasterization, native_ime_session, os_accessibility_publication, renderer_texture_upload_execution, clean_room_provenance, and external_engine_parity_non_claim. Required proof kinds are first_party_contract, official_sdk_adapter, audited_dependency_adapter, selected_package_counter, visible_editor_shell, host_gate, dependency_gate, and unsupported_non_claim. The evaluator rejects missing feature rows, duplicate row ids, public native handles, missing dependency records, host gates without explicit blockers, missing renderer upload execution proof, external Unity/Unreal/Godot parity claims, UI middleware exposure, copied external source/assets, selected rows that are not ready, invalid non-claim proof, and row budget overflow. tools/validate-runtime-ui-platform-production.ps1 -RequireReady is the supported wrapper for selected proof and requires package runtime_ui_platform_runtime_package_ready=1, package runtime_ui_platform_production_ready=0 before editor evidence is applied, runtime_ui_platform_clean_room_ready=1, zero external-engine parity/public-native-handle counters, selected directwrite/tsf/uia/d3d12 adapter counters, and editor_runtime_ui_editor_panel_visible=1 before emitting final runtime_ui_platform_production_ready=1. This is the production claim gate only; it accepts selected Windows DirectWrite text/font evidence, selected Windows TSF native IME session evidence, selected Windows UIA accessibility publication evidence, selected D3D12 UI atlas texture upload/readback evidence, and selected visible Runtime UI Editor shell evidence; it does not implement or mark native candidate UI, non-Windows IME sessions, non-Windows accessibility publication, full UIA/screen-reader parity, Vulkan/Metal renderer texture upload execution, external engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad platform UI readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-platform-adapter-gates
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
ui.notes: Runtime UI Platform Adapter Gates v1 exposes runtime_ui_platform_adapter_gate_rows as a fixed fail-closed ledger. The selected proof rows are runtime_ui.adapter.windows.directwrite, runtime_ui.adapter.windows.tsf, runtime_ui.adapter.windows.uia, and runtime_ui.upload.windows.d3d12. Dependency-gated rows are runtime_ui.adapter.linux.harfbuzz_fontconfig and runtime_ui.adapter.linux.freetype with blockers requiring explicit non-default audited dependency selection, vcpkg feature, and legal notices. Host-gated rows are runtime_ui.adapter.macos.core_text, runtime_ui.adapter.macos.input_method_kit, runtime_ui.adapter.macos.nsaccessibility, runtime_ui.adapter.linux.at_spi, runtime_ui.adapter.android.text_input, runtime_ui.adapter.android.accessibility, runtime_ui.adapter.ios.uitextinput, runtime_ui.adapter.ios.uiaccessibility, runtime_ui.upload.vulkan, and runtime_ui.upload.metal with fixed blocker strings. No optional dependencies are added by this row, and selected Windows/D3D12 proof must not imply non-Windows, Vulkan, Metal, candidate-UI, screen-reader parity, external-engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad platform UI readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-win32-directwrite-text-shaping
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_win32_ui_text_font_tests MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests MK_ui_renderer_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_win32_ui_text_font_tests|MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests|MK_ui_renderer_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
ui.notes: Runtime UI Windows DirectWrite Text Shaping Adapter v1 adds Win32UiTextShapeRequest, Win32UiTextShapeResult, Win32UiTextGlyphRow, Win32UiTextBoundaryRow, Win32UiTextFallbackFamilyRow, shape_win32_ui_text_with_directwrite, validate_win32_ui_text_shape_result_rows, and make_win32_directwrite_text_shaping_production_evidence in MK_platform_win32. The adapter uses Windows SDK DirectWrite through private IDWriteFactory, IDWriteTextLayout, IDWriteTextAnalyzer, and COM implementation details in src/win32_ui_text_font.cpp while the public header remains value-only. Selected evidence includes strict UTF-8 scalar cluster offsets, glyph ids, advances, offsets, fallback family rows, script/language rows, line-break rows, bidi rows, diagnostics, dependency_recorded=true, host_evidence_available=true, and public_native_handles_exposed=false. It can satisfy production_text_shaping with official_sdk_adapter on Windows only and must not imply Core Text, HarfBuzz/Fontconfig, real font loading/rasterization, native IME, non-Windows accessibility publication, full UIA/screen-reader parity, renderer texture upload execution, external engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad runtime/editor UI platform readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-win32-directwrite-font-rasterization
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_win32_ui_text_font_tests MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests MK_ui_renderer_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_win32_ui_text_font_tests|MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests|MK_ui_renderer_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset desktop-runtime --target sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; Push-Location out/build/desktop-runtime/games/Debug/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; .\sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.exe --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.geindex --require-runtime-ui-font-rasterization; Pop-Location; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
ui.notes: Runtime UI Windows DirectWrite Font Loading And Glyph Rasterization v1 adds Win32UiFontLoadRequest, Win32UiFontFaceRow, Win32UiGlyphRasterRequest, Win32UiGlyphRasterResult, Win32UiFontDiagnostic, load_win32_ui_font_face, rasterize_win32_ui_glyph, validate_win32_ui_font_load_result_rows, validate_win32_ui_glyph_raster_result_rows, make_win32_directwrite_font_loading_production_evidence, and make_win32_directwrite_font_rasterization_production_evidence in MK_platform_win32. The adapter uses the Windows SDK system font collection, font-face metrics, glyph design metrics, rendering parameters, glyph run analysis, and alpha texture generation through private DirectWrite/COM implementation details in src/win32_ui_text_font.cpp while the public header remains value-only. Selected evidence includes system font provenance rows, license status rows, resolved face ids, design units, glyph counts, alpha8 bitmap rows, glyph metrics rows, color-glyph check rows, diagnostics, dependency_recorded=true, host_evidence_available=true, and public_native_handles_exposed=false. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package can require --require-runtime-ui-font-rasterization and report one font loading row, one glyph raster row, one bitmap row, one metrics row, one font license row, zero native handles, and zero diagnostics on a supported Windows host. It can satisfy real_font_loading and font_rasterization with official_sdk_adapter on Windows only and must not imply bundled/distributable project font asset loading, Core Text, HarfBuzz/FreeType/Fontconfig, Android/iOS font adapters, full color glyph rendering, live glyph atlas generation, renderer texture upload execution, external engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad runtime/editor UI platform readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-win32-tsf-native-ime-session
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_win32_platform_tests MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_win32_platform_tests|MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset desktop-runtime --target sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; out/build/desktop-runtime/games/Debug/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.exe --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.geindex --require-runtime-ui-tsf-session; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
ui.notes: Runtime UI Windows TSF Native IME Session v1 adds Win32TsfTextSessionDesc, Win32TsfCompositionRow, Win32TsfCandidateIntentRow, Win32TsfTextAreaRow, Win32TsfTextSessionResult, plan_win32_tsf_text_session, and make_win32_tsf_native_ime_production_evidence in MK_platform_win32. The adapter uses the Windows Text Services Framework through private ITfThreadMgr, ITfDocumentMgr, ITextStoreACP, ITfContextOwnerCompositionSink, and COM implementation details in src/win32_text_input.cpp while the public header remains value-only. Selected evidence includes TSF thread manager availability, document manager availability, context availability, focus sink rows, text store lock rows, composition begin/update/end rows, committed text rows, candidate intent rows, text area/caret rows, diagnostics, dependency_recorded=true, host_evidence_available=true, and public_native_handles_exposed=false. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package can require --require-runtime-ui-tsf-session and report runtime_ui_tsf_session_ready=1, runtime_ui_tsf_composition_rows=3, runtime_ui_tsf_candidate_intent_rows=1, runtime_ui_tsf_text_area_rows=1, runtime_ui_ime_native_candidate_ui_ready=0, runtime_ui_ime_cross_platform_ready=0, and runtime_ui_tsf_diagnostics=0 on a supported Windows host. It can satisfy native_ime_session with official_sdk_adapter on Windows only and must not imply native candidate UI, reconversion, UI-less candidate rendering, virtual keyboard policy, Cocoa/InputMethodKit, IBus/Fcitx, Android/iOS IME adapters, non-Windows accessibility publication, full UIA/screen-reader parity, external engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad runtime/editor UI platform readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-win32-uia-accessibility-publication
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_win32_platform_tests MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_win32_platform_tests|MK_runtime_ui_platform_production_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset desktop-runtime --target sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; out/build/desktop-runtime/games/Debug/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.exe --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.geindex --require-runtime-ui-uia-publication; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
ui.notes: Runtime UI Windows UIA Accessibility Publication v1 adds Win32UiaRuntimeNodeRow, Win32UiaProviderPublicationDesc, Win32UiaProviderPublicationResult, publish_runtime_ui_to_win32_uia, and make_win32_uia_accessibility_publication_production_evidence in MK_platform_win32. The adapter uses Windows UI Automation through private IRawElementProviderSimple, IInvokeProvider, UiaClientsAreListening, UiaRaiseAutomationEvent, ProviderOptions_ServerSideProvider, and COM implementation details in src/win32_ui_accessibility.cpp while the public header remains value-only. Selected evidence includes provider root availability, role rows, name rows, description rows, state rows, focus rows, action rows, parent/child relationship rows, reading-order rows, live-region rows, keyboard-pattern rows, bounds rows, event-publication rows, diagnostics, dependency_recorded=true, host_evidence_available=true, public_native_handles_exposed=false, and cross_platform_accessibility_ready=false. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package can require --require-runtime-ui-uia-publication and report runtime_ui_uia_provider_ready=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_nodes=2, runtime_ui_accessibility_action_rows=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_event_rows=1, runtime_ui_accessibility_cross_platform_ready=0, runtime_ui_accessibility_native_handles_exposed=0, and runtime_ui_uia_diagnostics=0 on a supported Windows host. It can satisfy os_accessibility_publication with official_sdk_adapter on Windows only and must not imply NSAccessibility, AT-SPI2, Android/iOS accessibility, full screen-reader parity, full UIA pattern/event parity, external engine compatibility, visual parity, API parity, or broad runtime/editor UI platform readiness.

ui.name: runtime-ui-renderer-atlas-handoff
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package
ui.notes: Runtime UI Renderer Atlas Handoff v1 plus UI Renderer Execution v1 adds UiRendererAtlasHandoffRequest, UiRendererAtlasHandoffPlan, review_ui_renderer_atlas_handoff, UiRendererSubmission, UiRendererLayerRow, UiRendererClipRectRow, UiRendererScissorRow, UiRendererMaskReviewRow, UiRendererAtlasResidencyRef, UiRendererExecutionPlan, and plan_ui_renderer_execution for side-effect-free review of cooked image/glyph atlas metadata, atlas placement and budget rows, eviction diagnostics, texture-upload handoff review, deterministic layer/modal order, clip/scissor rows, reviewed mask rows, batch grouping rows, atlas residency refs, renderer submission counters, selected package evidence, unsupported broad claims, side-effect flags, and replay hashes. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-runtime-ui-renderer-atlas-handoff validates runtime/assets/2d/hud.uiatlas, one image atlas page/binding, one glyph atlas page/binding, two placement rows, two budget rows, one texture handoff row, one texture upload execution row, one renderer counter row, two renderer sprites submitted, ui_renderer_layers=1, ui_renderer_batches=1, ui_renderer_clip_rects=1, ui_renderer_unresolved_resources=0, ui_renderer_native_handles_exposed=0, zero unsupported claims, zero side effects, and a positive replay hash. The handoff review and execution plan remain side-effect-free; selected D3D12 upload execution is owned by the runtime-ui-atlas-upload-execution row. It is selected package evidence only, not runtime source image decoding, live glyph atlas generation, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, native/RHI handle exposure, Vulkan/Metal renderer upload execution, or broad runtime UI renderer quality.

ui.name: runtime-ui-atlas-upload-execution
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_rhi_tests MK_ui_renderer_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_runtime_rhi_tests|MK_ui_renderer_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset desktop-runtime --target sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package; out/build/desktop-runtime/games/Debug/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.exe --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package.geindex --require-runtime-ui-atlas-upload --require-runtime-ui-renderer-atlas-handoff
ui.notes: Runtime UI Atlas Upload Execution v1 adds RuntimeUiAtlasTexturePageUploadExpectation, RuntimeUiAtlasTextureUploadDesc, RuntimeUiAtlasTextureUploadResult, and execute_runtime_ui_atlas_texture_upload for cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 page metadata plus first-party texture payloads already present in a runtime package. The proof uses caller-owned RhiUploadRing staging, uploads the selected atlas page through upload_runtime_texture, writes a sampled texture descriptor, records upload buffer allocation, copy-to-texture, copy-to-readback, resource-transition, descriptor-write, fence, row-pitch, and checksum evidence, performs readback, and keeps owner-device evidence count-only with native_handle_accessed=false. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package can require --require-runtime-ui-atlas-upload and report runtime_ui_atlas_upload_ready=1, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_backend=d3d12, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_uploaded_bytes=256, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_readback_checksum_matched=1, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_sampled_descriptor_written=1, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_d3d12_selected_proof_ready=1, runtime_ui_renderer_texture_upload_public_api=0, runtime_ui_atlas_upload_native_handle_accessed=0, Vulkan/Metal host gates, and zero diagnostics. This is selected D3D12 UI atlas upload/readback evidence only; runtime source image decoding, live glyph atlas generation, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, public gameplay renderer upload APIs, public native/RHI handles, Vulkan/Metal upload execution, and broad runtime UI renderer quality remain unclaimed.

ui.name: native-gpu-runtime-ui-overlay
hosts: Vulkan runtime plus DXC SPIR-V CodeGen plus spirv-val for strict parity
ui.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1; tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_desktop_runtime_game; strict Vulkan package smoke with --require-native-ui-overlay on a ready host
ui.notes: First-party MK_ui_renderer box/image-placeholder sprite submissions can be routed through public mirakana::IRenderer::draw_sprite into a renderer-owned final native GPU overlay in the sample desktop runtime scene/postprocess/shadow package path. This is a colored rectangle/sprite-placeholder proof with backend-private shader/pipeline/buffer ownership and status fields, not production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlases, image decoding, real texture atlases, IME, OS accessibility bridges, Metal readiness, or general production renderer quality.

ui.name: MK_editor_ui
ui.notes: GUI-independent editor models back selected Dear ImGui shell panels without making Dear ImGui the production UI foundation.

physics.name: first-party-deterministic
physics.notes: 2D/3D broadphase, circle/sphere/capsule contacts, layers/masks, collision-bounds raycasts, trigger overlaps, conservative bounds-based shape sweeps, exact 3D AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule shape sweeps against current AABB/sphere/capsule targets through PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep, exact sphere convenience casts, deterministic contact manifolds through PhysicsContactPoint3D, PhysicsContactManifold3D, and PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds with stable feature ids, warm-start-safe point rows, trigger/disabled exclusion, and solver consumption for current AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule contacts, conservative 3D capsule character-controller movement, first-party in-memory and package-authored collision rows, iterative solver configuration, stacked-body coverage, replay tests, host-independent PhysicsReplaySignature3D rows, and count-based PhysicsDeterminismGate3DResult budget diagnostics through make_physics_replay_signature_3d and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d plus opt-in deterministic PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous CCD rows, pure generated-game character/dynamic policy rows through PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DDesc, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRow, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DResult, and evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d, and explicit distance-joint solving through PhysicsJointSolve3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_joints_3d define the validated first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface; opt-in distance/fixed/linear-axis positional constraint solving through PhysicsConstraintSolve3DDesc, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_constraints_3d is a post-1.0 first-party expansion; dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, rotational rigid-body constraints, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, vehicles, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends outside the 1.0 ready surface, middleware, oriented boxes, mesh/convex casts, timing benchmarks, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface are future work.

network.name: first-party-loopback-facade
network.notes: Runtime Network Transport Adapter Facade v1 validates one loopback-only caller-owned adapter exchange through RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeRequest, IRuntimeNetworkTransportAdapter, and execute_runtime_network_loopback_exchange. The facade fails closed before dispatch for missing or unavailable adapters, unsupported loopback/reliable/unreliable capabilities, native-handle requests, peer_count != 1, invalid channel counts/channels, payloads above the first-party ceiling, invalid service budgets, and adapter exceptions. MK_runtime does not open sockets, own native handles, implement encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, replication, rollback, or claim broad multiplayer readiness.

network.name: network-production-security-gate
network.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-network-enet.ps1
network.notes: Runtime Network Production Security Gate v1 validates threat-modeled local loopback, session lifecycle, connection state, channel policy, reliable/unreliable delivery rows, replication security evidence, sequence/replay rejection, input command validation, snapshot validation, and rollback-window diagnostics through plan_runtime_network_production_security_gate. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-gameplay-systems emits network_production_security_* counters with host-gated loopback/replication readiness and zero side effects. Optional ENet host evidence feeds the same gate through validate-network-enet. This is not encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, cloud/backend services, internet-facing execution, rollback execution, or broad online readiness.

network.name: enet-loopback
network.notes: Optional ENet adapter lane behind MK_ENABLE_NETWORK_ENET and the vcpkg network-enet feature. It keeps ENet private to engine/runtime/network/enet implementation sources, supports one local loopback client peer with reliable and unreliable packet rows, validates install/export through Mirakanai_HAS_NETWORK_ENET, and is not external network readiness, secure transport, matchmaking, NAT traversal, replication, rollback, or broad multiplayer readiness.

runtimeHost.name: win32-native-d3d12-foundation
runtimeHost.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_runtime_host_win32_tests MK_runtime_host_win32_public_api_compile; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_runtime_host_win32_tests|MK_runtime_host_win32_public_api_compile"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-native-desktop-contracts.ps1
runtimeHost.notes: Windows-native runtime-host foundation owns Win32 runtime/window/event-pump state, first-party input/pointer/gamepad/lifecycle rows, DesktopGameRunner wiring, deterministic NullRenderer fallback, D3D12/Vulkan RhiFrameRenderer creation from host-supplied shader bytecode, D3D12 swapchain planning rows, renderer/RHI stats reporting, scene GPU binding injection, postprocess, directional shadow, renderer quality, GPU memory, debug profiling, resize/present status rows, and no public SDL3/native window/SurfaceHandle exposure. The sample shell, non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game, committed generated desktop runtime package samples, and tools/new-game.ps1 desktop runtime templates now use this Win32 host. The optional native editor shell is validated separately through the desktop-editor lane and keeps Win32, DXGI, Direct3D 12, swapchain, descriptor heap, and fence handles private to editor implementation files.

runtimeHost.name: environment-quality-budget-package-evidence
runtimeHost.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_environment_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "MK_environment_tests"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/run-validation-recipe.ps1 -Mode Execute -Recipe desktop-runtime-sample-game-environment-audio-playback -HostGateAcknowledgements d3d12-windows-primary
runtimeHost.notes: Environment Production Excellence v1 Phase 8 proves fail-closed quality budget rows through EnvironmentQualityBudgetRequest, EnvironmentQualityBudgetUsageDesc, EnvironmentQualityBudgetLimitsDesc, EnvironmentQualityBudgetPlan, environment_quality_budget_preset_limits, evaluate_environment_quality_budget, and has_environment_quality_budget_diagnostic. Selected package counters include environment_quality_preset=high, environment_quality_budget_status=ready, environment_quality_budget_ready=1, environment_quality_budget_requested=1, environment_quality_budget_violations=0, environment_quality_budget_broad_optimization_claimed=0, and environment_quality_budget_broad_environment_ready_claimed=0 without backend profiler timing, broad optimization, backend parity, or broad environment_ready claims.

platform.name: headless
platform.notes: Virtual input, lifecycle, mobile lifecycle/touch/orientation/safe-area/storage/permission contracts, filesystem, process, file watcher selection, and deterministic memory/rooted services are the generated-game baseline.

platform.name: win32-native
platform.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 --build --preset dev --target MK_win32_platform_tests MK_runtime_ui_workbench_tests MK_runtime_ui_production_stack_tests; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ctest.ps1 --preset dev --output-on-failure -R "win32_platform|runtime_ui"; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-public-api-boundaries.ps1
platform.notes: Windows-native runtime, hidden native window creation, resize/move/close handling, display-state query, copied message pump rows, cursor-mode planning, keyboard/mouse/modifier translation into first-party input rows, keyboard/mouse-scoped Raw Input registration planning, CF_UNICODETEXT clipboard adapters, Common Item Dialog file open/save/folder service rows, and minimal UTF-16 committed text plus text-edit/clipboard shortcut rows are validated behind first-party MK_platform and MK_ui values. Desktop runtime host and presentation adoption is validated for the sample shell, non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game, committed generated desktop runtime package samples, and tools/new-game.ps1 desktop runtime templates. Controller execution, full TSF composition/candidate UI, and editor shell migration remain future focused work.

platform.name: mobile-contracts
platform.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1
platform.notes: SDK-independent mobile lifecycle, touch, orientation, safe-area, storage, and permission contracts are implemented; Android GameActivity and Apple package templates adapt into these contracts without exposing native handles.
